channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Crimson Desert Devs Have Added Denuvo Anti-Tamper
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has added Denuvo Anti-Tamper to Crimson Desert, and fans don’t seem to be happy about the decision.

Crimson Desert is all set to release for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC in a week on March 19, 2026, and they have released a launch trailer to build hype today. However, it seems the developers have also added third-party DRM to the game just a week before it arrives.

Crimson Desert Gets Denuvo A Week Ahead of Launch, and Some Fans Aren’t Happy

Spotted by The Gamer, if you head over to SteamDB and see Crimson Desert’s update history, you will see that the latest change today stated: “Added 3rd-Party DRM – Denuvi Anti-tamper, 5 different PC within a day machine activation limit.” This has seemingly sent some fans into a frenzy, with a full-fledged Steam discussion about players disappointed with the decision.

For those unaware, Denuvo Anti-tamper is software designed to prevent games from being pirated or cracked. However, there are claims that this software can affect a game’s performance, including reduced FPS, stuttering, and more. At the same time, some claim that it doesn’t impact the game.

Moreover, Pearl Abyss made several other changes to Crimson Desert’s Steam store listing, including changes to the store description, the trailers, screenshots, and some assets.

In other news, here’s Insider Gaming’s guide on Crimson Desert’s pre-order bonuses, game editions, prices, and more. Additionally, Pearl Abyss has fired back against doubters who claim they’re hiding elements. What are your thoughts on Crimson Desert getting Denuvo a week ahead of launch? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Pokémon Pokopia surpassing 2.2 million global sales in 4 days. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Super Meat Boy 3D Release Date Seemingly Revealed through Nintendo eShop
item.content:encoded:
According to the Nintendo eShop listing of Super Meat Boy 3D, the game is scheduled to release at the end of March 2026.

Team Meat revealed Super Meat Boy 3D during the Xbox Games Showcase last year, confirming the title would release in 2026 for Xbox Series X/S, PlayStation 5, and PC. We also received a brand-new trailer that showcased fresh 3D-perspective gameplay, along with new stages, obstacles, and more. Now, it seems players will be able to play it by the end of this month.

Super Meat Boy 3D Releases on the Last Day of March 2026

If you head over to the Nintendo eShop right now, you will see that Super Meat Boy 3D lists a release date, seemingly March 31, 2026. Now, there is some speculation that this could be a placeholder for the game, as it is the last day of March. However, there’s another side to the story, where players think that the game’s release date might have been accidentally revealed ahead of the official announcement at the Future Games Show later today.

In Super Meat Boy 3D, you get to play as an animated cube of meat, who is trying to save his girlfriend, who is made of bandages from “an evil fetus in a jar wearing a tux.” The game features the old-school difficulty of retro titles, where you get to indulge in “tough-as-nails precision platforming action.” You will explore forests that could be on fire, dumps filled with the waste of mankind, and high-tech forges that are responsible for the traps that kill Meat Boy regularly.

In other news, check out Insider Gaming’s hands-on preview of Super Meat Boy 3D from Gamescom 2025. What are your thoughts on Super Meat Boy 3D’s release date accidentally surfacing online? Do you think it is a placeholder? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Crimson Desert’s launch trailer, showcasing some epic boss fights that players should expect. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Virtus.pro Promote Academy Players Following Calamitous Year
item.content:encoded:
Virtus.pro has promoted Dias ‘F0R3VER’ Kutubay and Alexander ‘AquaRS’ Kovalev to its main Counter-Strike roster as the organisation looks to rebuild after a disastrous period that saw the organisation go from Tier-1 relevancy to #123 in the world in the latest VRS snapshot.

The move follows the benching of Petr ‘fame’ Bolyshev, Evgenii ‘FL1T’ Lebedev, and Ilya ‘Perfecto’ Zalutskiy in a roster that showed no signs of potential and delivered zero results despite the roster featuring the three major winners.

Young guns give Virtus.pro a rankings boost

F0R3VER and AquaRS rejoin fellow VP.Prodigy graduates Vadim ‘tO0RO’ Arkov and AWPer Vladimir ‘b1st’ Krasikov in the roster, giving the team both continuity as a roster but also, crucially, continuity as a core in VRS.

Their reunion means that Virtus.pro is no longer operating on the VRS core of the previous roster, but of the academy team, jumping the organisation in the rankings up to #77, according to the live version of the VRS rankings on HLTV.

While the rank is still a million miles from where Virtus.pro, one of Counter-Strike’s most historic organisations, wants to be, it is still a substantial jump from the previous roster.

There will be no loss of chemistry within the roster, due to the recently of the players’ time together. Despite their previous ‘promotion,’ tO0RO and b1st played in VP.Prodigy as recently as Wednesday, where they finished 5th-8th in Russian LAN, MPKBK CIS LAN Season 3.

While the four are match fit, Virtus.pro’s veteran element in Nikolay ‘mir’ Bityukov certainly isn’t. The 30-year-old Russian has not played an HLTV match since April 2024, almost two years ago.

Even with mir’s relatively advanced age, the roster has an average age of just 20. Under mir’s leadership, they will be hoping to develop further.

While VP.Prodigy’s ranking never broke through to high-level competition, the young team did score a notable victory against a struggling Liquid at the RES Showdown last August.

For Virtus.pro, the question of returning to Counter-Strike’s top table may not be measured in weeks, but years, as they bank on their young roster to grow over time.

Virtus.pro are now:

Dias ‘F0R3VER’ Kutubay
Alexander ‘AquaRS’ Kovalev
Nikolay ‘mir’ Bityukov
Vadim ‘tO0RO’ Arkov
Vladimir ‘b1st’ Krasikov

What do you think of the roster moves? Let us know on our community Discord!

To see the latest Counter-Strike news, see the ESL Pro League playoff bracket here.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Thursday, March 12
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 12)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 12, 2026.

Across

1 – Like jerk chicken and chicken vindaloo

6 – Capital of Vietnam

7 – “Well, would ya look at that!”

8 – Gem in an oyster

9 – Thick roll of cash

Down

1 – Ship

2 – The “P” in I.P.A.

3 – Relative by marriage

4 – King ___ (venomous snake)

5 – Sign obeyed by merging traffic

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 12)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 12, 2026.

Across

1 – Hot, when eating

6 – Used to be ‘Thang Long’

7 – Expression of surprise or shock

8 – The Black ___, Pirates of the Caribbean ship

9 – Small, compressed mass of soft material

Down

1 – Another word for boat

2 – Dark opposite

3 – Wife’s sibling, your sister ____

4 – Hooded snake

5 – To bend the knee

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 12)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 12, 2026.

Across

1 – SPICY

6 – HANOI

7 – ILLBE

8 – PEARL

9 – WAD

Down

1 – SHIP

2 – PALE

3 – INLAW

4 – COBRA

5 – YIELD

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more word game hints, take a look at today’s Contexto, and check out the answers for today’s Connections.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xtreme Gaming, Parivision, and NAVI Miss Out on PGL Wallachia Season 7 Playoffs
item.content:encoded:
PGL Wallachia Season 7’s playoffs are underway after the conclusion of the Swiss Stage, which saw Xtreme Gaming, Parivision, and NAVI fall narrowly short of the playoffs.

Vici Gaming, BetBoom, and Tundra joined Liquid, Spirit, Heroic, Aurora, and Yandex in the playoffs for the second PGL Wallachia of the 2025/26 DotA 2 season.

Disappointing end for fan-favourite orgs

TI 2025 runners-up, Xtreme Gaming, fell to regional rivals Vici Gaming. VG, while a legendary organisation in DotA 2, only returned to the DotA circuit in October following its departure in 2023.

The regional victory, combined with wins over OG and Falcons, shows that Vici may be a serious contender already despite their short time since returning as they look to recapture their historic success with Guo ‘Xm’ Hongcheng.

The 2-3 elimination was a disappointing follow-up for NAVI after their BLAST Dota Slam VI runners-up placement, which saw them top the Round Robin standings, in a run that included wins against MOUZ, Falcons, and Tundra, before eventually falling to Liquid in the Grand Final.

While NAVI did once again take Tundra down at Wallachia, but their run was brutally ended by BetBoom, who swept NAVI aside in two 33-minute contests.

Parivision’s mediocre start to the year continued with their elimination to Tundra. The Russian team still seems to be finding its feet following the departure of coach Filipe ‘Astini’ Astini Ribeiro and the installation of Clement ‘Puppey’ Ivanov in his place.

Wins against Yellow Submarine and NAVI were followed by the nightmare scenario of three losses in a row against Spirit, Yandex, and Tundra in a hard-fought series that averaged over 50 minutes per game.

PGL Wallachia Season 7 Playoffs are already underway with BetBoom taking down Heroic 2-0 to send them to the lower bracket. Elsewhere, VG takes on Team Spirit, Aurora plays Tandex, and Tundra will face BLAST DotA Slam VI winners, Team Liquid.

Who do you think will take home the title at PGL Wallachia Season 7? Discuss that and more on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more Valve-related news, see the latest on the lawsuits filed against the Valve Corporation.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
